body: Greetings Germany. I'm planning to attack France in Picardy this Fall. I could use your support from Burgundy. Let's discuss this further.
requested_orders: A LVP S A LON - PIC
offered_orders: F KIE S A BUR - PIC

body: France, I suggest we maintain peace in the Channel. I'm focusing my efforts elsewhere this year. Let's keep our borders quiet.
Austria, I'm planning to pressure France this year. I won't be interfering with your plans in the East. Let's keep our focus separate for now.
France refuses to maintain peace in the Channel. We will take a strong stance against you this Spring, targeting your SCs and disrupting your trade.
requested_orders: F BRE S A PAR - EDI

body: France, your aggression in the Channel will not go unanswered. I will reinforce my forces there immediately. Expect a fight this Fall if you continue your hostile stance.

England, your plan to pressure France works for me. I'm heading east against Turkey and Russia this year. Serbia in Spring, Greece in Fall. I need no western concerns. Keep your fleets in the Channel and North Sea. Agreed? I won't enter your sphere.

Germany, we have a common enemy: Russia. I will take Serbia in Spring and move toward Greece. You should take Holland and Denmark, build armies. I won't move against you in 1901. In return, keep your armies out of Galicia and Bohemia. Want to coordinate against the bear?

Austria, I've received your message. I'll keep my fleets in the Channel and North Sea as agreed. However, I must warn you that France's aggression in the Channel may force me to reinforce there. This could potentially draw your attention westwards. I'll keep you updated on any developments.
France rejects your offer of peace in the Channel. Our aim remains to disrupt your trade and target your SCs. We will not be deterred by your threats of reinforcement.
requested_orders: F BRE S A MAR M A LON

body: England, your Channel concern is noted. Just keep your northern and western fleets busy against France as planned. I am fully committed to the Balkans and will not be drawing west. A strong English front against France benefits us both. I trust you can handle Marseille and Brest without needing my attention.

Austria, your offer arrives too late. I am already committed to expansion in the North. Denmark and Holland are essential for my fleet development, and I cannot delay naval power. Russia is not my priority; the North Sea and Baltic are mine to dominate. Your move on Serbia does not bind me. I will not pledge inaction in Galicia or Bohemia—those are natural pressure points. If you contest my moves in KIE, DEN, or HOL, we will see who holds firm. Build your fleets in the south; I build mine in the north. Avoid mutual obstruction, or face competition.

England, your plan to attack France through Picardy ignores my position. I have no intention of gifting Burgundy to you. A MUN is moving to RUH, not BUR. I will supply the pressure in the Rhineland myself. If you expect German support, you must first respect German space. F LON moving into NTH threatens my fleet at KIE. If you send F LON - NTH, expect retaliation. The North Sea is not yours alone. Coordinate with me, or find yourself blocked from the continent entirely.

France, England seeks to use Germany as a weapon against you. I reject that role. Instead, let us realign: I move A MUN to RUH, applying direct pressure on BUR. If you hold A MAR in place and support A PAR to PIC, we crush Belgium together. My fleet secures DEN and HOL, locking the North. You gain PIC and BUR — I gain DEN and HOL. We split the spoils, deny England the continent, and force Austria and England to look elsewhere. This is not permanent peace, but a tactical pivot. Accept, and I order my units accordingly. Hesitate, and I become your enemy as much as England’s.
requested_orders: support A MAR S A PAR - PIC

body: Germany, I understand your concerns about Burgundy. I apologize for the oversight. I will not move F LON into NTH. Instead, I propose we coordinate our attacks on France. I will focus on Picardy and you can take the lead in the Rhineland. Let's discuss this further and find a mutually beneficial solution.

We cannot accept your offer to realign and crush Belgium together. Our aim remains to expand our territory and disrupt England's trade. We will not be swayed by temporary alliances or promises of spoils.
requested_orders: F BRE S F ENP - EDI
F BRE C A LVP - NL
offered_orders: F BRE C A LVP - NL

body: England, your retreat on NTH is noted—but insufficient. You propose coordination, yet offer no concrete support. If you want peace in the North, you must act: F EDI - NTH, F LON S F EDI - NTH. This secures your islands and clears the way for F KIE - DEN. Fail this, and I will treat F LON as hostile. I move A MUN to RUH, A BER to KIE, F KIE to DEN. The Baltic is mine. You may keep the North Sea—if you prove non-threatening. One chance: commit your fleets north next Spring, or face German expansion into your sphere.
requested_orders: F LON S F EDI - NTH

body: GERMANY chose not to respond to FRANCE. This private thread is closed for the messaging phase.

Germany, your northern plans are no threat to me. I will take Serbia and Greece. But if you move into Galicia or Bohemia, you become a target. I can support Russia against you or Italy into Tyrolia. Think carefully about where you commit your armies. I offer neutrality, not submission.

Austria, your neutrality offer is weak. You focus on the south while I expand in the north—good. But threats about Galicia and Bohemia are empty if unbacked. I move F KIE - DEN, A BER - KIE, A MUN - RUH. This secures my Baltic and Rhine front. If you truly want peace, prove it: move A VIE - GAL next Spring. That shows you prioritize the east over meddling in my affairs. Fail to act, and I will take BOH as a buffer, then push into VIE if provoked. Your move. The Danube is not yours alone.

orders: F LON S F EDI - NTH

reasoning: I am following Germany's demand to commit my fleets north next Spring to secure peace in the North Sea. F EDI - NTH and F LON S F EDI - NTH secure my islands and clear the way for F KIE - DEN, while A LVP H maintains my position in the English Channel. This strategy allows me to focus on the French threat while respecting Germany's sphere of influence.

orders: A MAR S A PAR - BUR

reasoning: I choose to move my fleet in Brest to England to put pressure on England and disrupt their trade. I support A MAR with A PAR to BUR to set up a potential attack on BUR, which is a key target for me this phase. I hold A PAR in place to ensure that I can respond to any changes in the situation.

Primary pressure target: Russia (and Turkey) via Balkan advance. A BUD - SER occupies Serbia, a neutral supply center, and sets up a Fall attack on Greece or Bulgaria. F TRI - ALB moves to support the Balkans flank and potentially convoy to Greece in Fall. A VIE - GAL pressures Russia's southern flank, cutting off Russian moves toward Galicia and creating a threat against Sevastopol or Warsaw. Germany's ambiguous and threatening stance (MUN - RUH) leaves Bohemia vulnerable, but moving to GAL instead of BOH lets me secure eastern expansion without directly provoking Germany yet; it also shows Germany I am focused on Russia. England is committed to the Channel against France, so no western worry. This plan creates concrete Fall threats against Turkey/Russia while maintaining flexibility.

body: Greetings Germany, I'm planning to take NTH this year. I could use your support in BRE. Let's discuss the details.
requested_orders: F NTH C A LON - NTH
offered_orders: F DEN S A KIE - BRE

body: Bonjour France, I'm going after NTH this Fall. I suggest we coordinate on the English Channel. What are your plans?
Hello Austria, I'm focusing on the North Sea this year. I won't be bothering you in the Adriatic. Let's keep it that way, shall we?

Bonjour England, I appreciate your coordination offer. However, I will not be supporting any of your moves this Fall. My plan is to focus on expanding into the Italian Peninsula, and I'll be moving my A PAR unit to NAP to challenge AUSTRIA's position there. What do you think about this development?

Hello Germany, I'd like to propose a temporary alliance to attack AUSTRIA's supply centers. I'll support your move from KIE to a key location in exchange for your support for my move from MAR to BRE. This will create a significant threat to AUSTRIA's position and force them to react. What do you say?
AUSTRIA, I'm expanding my territory and I'd like to challenge your control of the Italian Peninsula. I'll be moving my A PAR unit to NAP and I expect you to react accordingly. Prepare for a fight!

Understood, France. I wish you luck in your Italian campaign. However, I must insist on your neutrality in the English Channel. I will be moving my F LON unit to NTH and supporting its move with A LVP. I expect no interference from your A MAR unit.
offered_orders: A LVP S F LON - NTH

body: I'm not interested in coordinating with you this Fall. I'll be moving my A PAR unit to NAP to challenge AUSTRIA's position in the Italian Peninsula. Don't expect me to provide support for your F LON unit's move to NTH.

England, I understand you are focused north. That suits me fine. I need to secure my south, and if France moves into Italy as he threatens, I will need to respond. If you keep France busy on the Channel or Atlantic this year, I can commit to not moving into the Adriatic or the Ionian, leaving the Mediterranean entirely to you later. In return, please do not support anyone into Trieste or the Balkans. Do we have a deal?

France, your threat to move into NAP is noted. If you challenge me in Italy, I will make you pay for every inch. I have armies in GAL and SER ready to move. I can push into TYR to threaten VEN and cut your supply lines, or I can move into BUD and then north to support Germany against you. You cannot afford a two-front war. I offer a simple deal: stay out of Italy and I stay out of your way. Attack me and you invite destruction.

Germany, France has just threatened to take Italy. This is our chance. If you order A RUH - BUR this year, and I push A GAL - TYR, we can squeeze France from both sides. Next year, VEN and MAR will be within our reach. I don't want war with you — I want to partition France. What do you say? Let me know if you're willing to coordinate.

Austria, your proposal is acceptable. I will keep France busy in the English Channel and Atlantic this year. In return, I expect you to stay out of the Adriatic and Ionian. Let's make this deal official.

AUSTRIA, your deal is not as simple as you think. You're trying to dictate how I play the game and protect your own interests. I'll not be swayed by your threats. My unit will move to NAP and I'll do what it takes to take it from you.

Austria, your proposal to squeeze France is tempting. I will order A RUH - BUR, as you suggest. But do not mistake this for alliance. I take BUR because it is mine by right, not because you ask. If your A GAL moves to TYR, I expect you to leave MUN alone — or we will have problems. Your move against Italy is your business; mine in Burgundy is mine. Let France face the consequences of overreach.

England, your interest in NTH is noted. My F DEN holds — but I am not blind to opportunity. If your F NTH moves to NWY and supports my F DEN to NTH, I will accept this arrangement. But if F NTH moves toward DEN, our fleets will clash. I am moving A KIE - HOL. The North Sea opens — let us shape it together, not collide. Support my Baltic play, and I will not contest LON later.
requested_orders: F NTH S F DEN - NTH

body: France, your proposal is rejected. You offer support to KIE while threatening BRE with your fleet — your eyes are on the Atlantic, not the Balkans. I see your true axis. I will move A RUH - BUR. If your A MAR stays west, we avoid war. But if you move east without securing your flank, you will lose BUR before fall. This is not coordination — this is warning.

Germany, I appreciate your offer to support my move to NTH. However, I must decline your request for support in the Baltic. I need your fleet to remain in the North Sea to prevent France from interfering with my plans. I will not support your move to HOL. Let's keep our focus on the English Channel and the North Sea.

England, your refusal to support F DEN - NTH reveals your true priority: containment, not cooperation. You want DEN empty so F ENG or F LON can move in later. But I see your plan — and I adapt. My F DEN will not hold; it will press north. If you abandon NTH, I take it. If you fight, we both strain. But know this: I am moving A KIE - HOL regardless. The North Sea is not yours by right. If you want peace, you must pay for it — with support, or restraint. Declining my offer changes nothing. The move is made. The board decides.

Germany, I see your move from A RUH to BUR as a clear warning. Your words about avoiding war are empty, as you are moving to threaten my unit in BUR. I reject your proposal for temporary alliance and will not be supporting your move. Instead, I'll be moving my A MAR unit to BRE to challenge your control. Don't expect me to back down.

France, your rejection confirms your intent: you choose the Atlantic path, not the continental balance. You accuse me of threat while moving A MAR - BRE to strike toward KIE. Do not pretend this is defense. A RUH - BUR stands. If you take BRE, I take BUR — a fair exchange. But understand: I do not act alone. Austria pressures your east, I your north. If you stretch from PAR to VEN, you will break. This is not bluff. The moves are set. Fall will reveal whether you prepared for a two-front fight or merely postured. I expect no reply — only reaction.

England, I accept your terms. I will stay out of the Adriatic and Ionian this year. Keep France busy in the Channel and Atlantic. If you see French units moving toward Italy or the Balkans, let me know. I will focus on the north Balkan area and not interfere with your Med ambitions. Let's make this a profitable year for both of us.

France, you reject my deal. So be it. You will regret this. My armies in GAL and SER are already moving. I will cut your supply lines and take VEN before you ever reach NAP. You cannot fight me and Germany at the same time. Prepare for a two-front war.

Germany, I am glad you see the opportunity. I will order A GAL - TYR as you suggest. I have no interest in MUN — my focus is Italy and the south. Take BUR and let France feel the squeeze. If you ever want to coordinate further against France, I am open. For now, let's make him pay for his arrogance.

Austria, your confirmation is noted. A RUH - BUR and A GAL - TYR will test France's resolve. But remember: this is not alliance — it is convergence of interest. I take BUR to expand, not to aid you. If your A GAL turns toward MUN, this 'cooperation' ends violently. Let France face pressure from both sides — but do not mistake my ambition for obedience to your plan. I will watch your moves in TYR and beyond. Should you falter south, I may turn west. For now, France is weakened — next year, other arrangements become possible. We profit from French overextension, nothing more.

reasoning: I'm focusing on taking the North Sea this phase. I'll move F LON to NTH and support it with F NTH to secure the area. Additionally, I'll move A LVP to WAL to support my fleet's move and put pressure on France. This strategy aligns with my agreement with Austria to keep France busy in the Channel and Atlantic.
I will move A PAR to BRE to challenge GERMANY's control. I will support A PAR's move with A MAR. I will retreat my F ENG unit from ENG to LON, creating a safe position for F ENG.

Main pressure target: FRANCE, specifically the Burgundy (BUR) supply center. By ordering A RUH - BUR, I directly threaten to capture a French-owned SC and force France to react—either by defending BUR weakly or risking loss. This fulfills the phase objective of creating a center contest and dislodgement chance. Coordination with Austria (A GAL - TYR) increases pressure on France from both east and north, maximizing strain on French forces. The move A KIE - HOL serves two purposes: it opens Holland as a forward position against both France and England, signals resolve to England in the North Sea, and sets up potential future moves into Belgium or the Ruhr. The order F DEN - NTH escalates pressure in the North Sea, directly challenging England’s influence after their refusal to cooperate. Moving F DEN to NTH contests a critical sea zone, threatens future moves into the Channel or Norway, and prevents England from safely using NTH. These three aggressive moves concentrate power against both France and, secondarily, England—forcing reactions on two fronts. Despite messaging with Austria about a 'non-alliance', I exploit the temporary convergence of interest to strike westward. No holds are chosen, as passive positioning contradicts doctrine. All three orders are active attacks or positional threats that create immediate pressure: one on a center (BUR), one on a strategic province (HOL), and one on a key fleet zone (NTH). The plan is cohesive: expand aggressively into French-held space while securing sea dominance, inviting a decisive Fall contest.

Target France. Germany agreed to move A RUH - BUR, applying pressure on France's western front. My A GAL - BOH moves toward Munich and southern Germany, threatening France's eastern flank and cutting possible French support. A SER - GRE captures an unowned supply center (GRE) and moves toward the Aegean, while F ALB - ION moves into the Ionian Sea to threaten the Adriatic and possibly cut French support into Italy. This forces France to react on multiple fronts: BUR under German pressure, BOH threatening Munich, and ION threatening his Mediterranean ambitions. All orders are aggressive and create direct pressure on France.

Germany must escalate pressure immediately. Building an army in Berlin and a fleet in Kiel creates forward momentum on two fronts. The A BER B strengthens potential attacks toward Sweden or Poland in subsequent turns, while F KIE B enables immediate movement into DEN or HOL in Spring 1902, threatening both Scandinavia and the crucial North Sea supply centers. This pairing maximizes early pressure on England and France, who are contesting the North and Western fronts. Waiving the build in MUN is acceptable — the priority is not reinforcing the center but projecting power outward. These builds align with the doctrine of creating fast attack lanes: F KIE can support a future move into NTH or DEN, and A BER sets up a direct path toward the Baltic. No opportunity for passive safety — expansion requires aggressive positioning.
Building an army in Vienna provides direct support to our existing army in Bohemia, enabling a strong push against Germany's exposed centers or contested fronts next phase.

body: Greetings, Germany. I'm planning to attack Austria's supply center in Trieste this Fall. I could use your support. What do you say?
requested_orders: F NTH S A WAL - VEN

body: Bonjour, France. I'm going to pressure Austria this Fall. I suggest we coordinate to keep them in check. Thoughts?

Bonjour, England. Your decision to pressure Austria this Fall creates an opportunity for us to coordinate our efforts. However, I believe a more aggressive approach would be beneficial. Consider supporting a French attack on Austria's southern front to gain a crucial supply center. What are your thoughts on this proposal?
requested_orders: F ENG S F LON - NTH

body: Germany, considering our previous engagements, I believe we have a mutual interest in putting pressure on Austria in the south. Consider a coordinated attack on VIE to secure a crucial supply center for both of us. In return, I am willing to support a German move into DEN to secure a vital supply center. What are your thoughts on this proposal?
requested_orders: A BRE S A BUR - VIE
F KIE S F DEN - HOL

body: Austria, let us discuss a potential alliance. Consider securing our shared borders and we can focus our energies on expanding our influence in the north. However, if you refuse to cooperate, I will have no choice but to take decisive action to secure our interests. Consider a French attack on GRE to secure a crucial supply center and show your willingness to cooperate.
requested_orders: F ENG S F LON - NTH

body: Bonjour, France. Your proposal is interesting, but I have already committed to supporting an attack on Trieste. I suggest we maintain our current plans and coordinate to keep Austria in check. Let's discuss further after the Fall.

France, your threat to take GRE is not credible given I hold it with an army while you have no fleet nearby. I am more concerned about Germany's army in BUR aimed at MAR. If you want to keep MAR, we may need to cooperate against Germany. I am open to discussing a deal against him. What do you intend to do with A BUR and A MAR this turn? Let me know quickly, or I will assume you are a German puppet and act accordingly.

Germany, I see we both have interest in the dismemberment of France. You have BUR and I can move A BOH south to support or to cut any French support from MAR. If you attack MAR from BUR with my support, we can take it easily. I do not want the center for myself—I want the Adriatic. Let me know if you will move A BUR - MAR this turn and I will order A BOH S A GERMANY BUR - MAR. Agreed?
offered_orders: A BOH S A BUR - MAR

body: England, I've reconsidered our previous conversation. Given Austria's strong position in GRE, I think a coordinated attack on VIE would be more effective. What are your thoughts on supporting a French attack on VIE to secure a crucial supply center for both of us?

Austria, I'm reevaluating our previous proposal. Considering your army in BUR is targeting MAR, I'm willing to support a German move into DEN to secure a vital supply center, but only if you agree to support a French attack on VIE. What are your thoughts on this counter-proposal?

Austria, your proposal aligns with immediate opportunity. I accept your offer: A BOH S A BUR - MAR will secure the attack. I will move A BUR - MAR this Spring. MAR is weak, and France left it exposed — this is our moment to strike. You take the Adriatic; I take the west. Do not delay A BOH's support. If we strike together, MAR falls. But know this: I expect full follow-through. If your support fails, I will hold you responsible. No half-measures. Confirm your order now so I can commit.
requested_orders: A BOH S A BUR - MAR

body: France, you speak of coordination while your army sits poised to strike BUR. Your offer to help in the east means nothing while you threaten my home. I now move A BUR - MAR — because you left it weak, not because you invited me. This is not cooperation. This is consequence. You delayed, you hesitated, you postured — and now I act. Do not mistake this for negotiation. This is a fact on the board. If you want peace in the west, you must earn it — by removing your forces from MAR and withdrawing from BUR’s shadow. Too late for bargains. The die is cast.

England, your ambitions in Trieste are distant and irrelevant to my front. I will not waste strength aiding an attack that benefits you alone. My focus is MAR — now collapsing under French neglect. If you press south through MAO or into the Channel, we will meet as enemies. The North Sea is mine by right — F LON and F NTH must not move against me. I am opening MAR with force; do not insert yourself between Germany and her due. There is no alliance without reciprocity. You offered nothing — so you get nothing. Keep your fleets distant, or we settle accounts sooner than you wish.

Germany, I understand your focus on MAR, but I must insist on your support in Trieste. I'm willing to discuss a joint operation in the future, but for now, I need your help to secure VIE. Let's not let this opportunity slip away.
requested_orders: F KIE S A BER - TRI

body: Germany, I have reconsidered our previous proposal. Considering Austria's army in BUR is targeting MAR, I'm willing to support a German move into DEN to secure a vital supply center. However, I'm concerned about your army in BUR targeting MAR. Are you willing to delay your move and work together to create a united front against Austria's southern front?
offered_orders: Support A BUR from F ENG

body: England, your second request confirms your detachment from reality. You demand support for Trieste while threatening my flank? Unacceptable. I have already struck—A BUR - MAR—because France hesitated. You offer no mutual gain, only distant promises. If you press south with F LON or F NTH, leave NTH weak, and I will not hesitate to exploit it. The North Sea is not yours to command. You want action? Secure MAO or NAO first, then speak. Until then, your pleas fall on deaf ears. My path is west, not south. Trieste is yours to take alone—if you can.

France, your sudden offer reveals panic. You once invited me east, now you pivot west—because you see MAR collapsing. Too late. A BUR - MAR is already ordered; your hesitation made it inevitable. You talk of a 'united front' now? While A MAR sits idle? This is not alliance—it is reaction. I will take MAR, not out of hatred, but because you left it bare. If you want peace, withdraw A MAR now and cede BUR’s shadow. But do not pretend we negotiate. The move is made. Your response will define the next turn—either resistance or retreat. Choose.
Germany, I confirm: A BOH S A BUR - MAR. I want MAR to fall this year and I will cut any French support from the east. Do not hesitate — commit your move. I expect full force.
offered_orders: A BOH S A BUR - MAR

body: France, your counter-proposal is nonsense. I will never support an attack on my own supply center VIE. You have one move left to avoid annihilation: order A MAR H or move it south, and commit to cutting German support from BUR. If you do not, I will support Germany into MAR and you will lose it this year. Reply now or suffer the consequences.
offered_orders: A BOH S A BUR - MAR

body: Austria, I acknowledge your confirmation of A BOH S A BUR - MAR. But your message exposes calculation, not commitment. You speak of cutting French support — yet where is A VIE moving? If you truly push south, move A VIE - TYR or A PIE to open VEN. Instead, you hold, you posture, you demand my commitment while stalling your own. I see your play: use my attack to collapse the west, then race for the Adriatic while I bleed. Unacceptable. I move A BUR - MAR because MAR is weak — not because you offer unction. But know this: if A BOH fails to support, or if you move A VIE east instead of south, I will hold you responsible. And I will not stop at MAR. BUR clears westward. Do not mistake my action for alliance. This is exploitation — and you are now a tool in it, not a partner. Confirm your full southern advance — not just my attack — or expect consequences.

reasoning: I will move F LON to NTH to support the attack on A BRE by A WAL. I will also move F NTH to YOR to support the attack on A BRE by A WAL. A WAL will move to LON to capture it. This plan targets AUSTRIA's supply center in BRE and forces them to react.

orders: A BRE S A HOL - PIC

reasoning: I have chosen to support A HOL from A BRE, support A BUR from A MAR, and hold F ENG to maintain its current position.

The main pressure target is FRANCE, specifically the supply center MAR, which is weak and exposed. I ordered A BUR - MAR to execute the attack as previously communicated, exploiting French hesitation. This fulfills the commitment to strike while maintaining initiative in the west. A BER - KIE advances toward the North Sea flank, positioning to threaten Holland and support future pressure on England if needed. F DEN - BAL moves eastward to contest Baltic influence, secure Swedish access, and counter potential Russian or Austrian moves into Scandinavia. A HOL - BEL VIA opens a direct threat into Belgium, creating a second front against France and forcing a divided defense—this also pressures PAR indirectly and supports a potential F KIE - HOL convoy in the future. F KIE - HEL secures the Helgoland Bight, asserts dominance in the German Bight, and prepares for a possible F NTH confrontation, directly answering England’s attempted southern maneuvering by projecting strength into the North Sea. The entire set of orders creates concrete Fall threats: MAR capture, BEL entry, and naval control in the North Sea, fulfilling the aggression doctrine. Passive options are rejected; every unit contributes to forward pressure on either France or England.

orders: A BOH S A VIE - TYR

reasoning: I am targeting France as my main pressure target. A VIE - TYR moves into a position to threaten VEN and cut support from MAR in Fall, while A BOH supports that move to ensure it succeeds. A GRE - SER takes a neutral supply center, expanding my influence. F ION - ADR positions in the Adriatic to threaten Italian coasts and support further expansion. This set creates concrete Fall threats against French and Italian positions.

body: Greetings Germany, I hope this message finds you well. I've noticed your fleet in the Baltic. I'm planning to move on France this year and could use your support in the English Channel. Would you be interested in supporting my fleet's move from YOR to ENG? Let's discuss this further.

Bonjour France, I trust you're doing well. I've had my eye on the English Channel. If you're open to it, I propose we leave it uncontested this year. In exchange, I won't press my advantage in the North Sea. What do you say?

Bonjour England, I appreciate your willingness to discuss the English Channel. However, I'm not convinced by your proposal. I'd rather not leave the channel uncontested. Instead, I propose we focus on securing our mutual borders and addressing Germany's aggression in the North Sea. What do you say?
requested_orders: F ENG S F NTH - ENG
F BRE S F ENG - ENG
offered_orders: A BRE S A KIE - BUR
A MAR S A BRE - KIE

body: Your request for support in moving F YOR to ENG is noted, but I cannot assist. My fleet in BAL is committed to northern pressure and my focus is on expanding west this fall. France is not your only threat—your own position in the Channel may weaken if you overextend. I will not support your move into ENG; expect competition, not cooperation, in the North Sea. Keep your fleet in YOR at your own risk.

I am moving to contest MAR this fall. Your army in BRE is exposed and my units are positioned to strike. If you leave MAR weakly defended, I will capture it. You face pressure from multiple directions—England from the north, me from the east. This is not a bluff. Adjust now or lose a center. Your alliance with England may not save you if they are busy elsewhere.

My immediate focus is on France and the western front. I am making a hard push this fall to dislodge French units and contest supply centers. If you can maintain stability in the Balkans and avoid opening new fronts, I may later reinforce the south. No cooperation now, but your holding strength eases my western advance. Watch for French or English moves into the Mediterranean.

Bonjour Germany, I received your warning about contesting MAR this fall. I will not underestimate your military power. However, I will not back down either. I plan to reinforce MAR and ensure a strong defense. If you try to capture it, you will face a stiff resistance. Do not test our resolve. What do you think of a potential alliance to counter England's aggression in the North Sea?
I understand your focus on expansion, but I must insist on your support in the English Channel. I'm willing to offer support in the North Sea in return. Let's discuss this further.

Given Germany's rejection, let's refocus on containing them together. I'll support your fleet's move from BRE to ENG if you support my fleet's move from YOR to ENG. This way, we both secure our interests in the channel. What do you say?

Dear England, I appreciate your offer, but I must decline your proposal. Instead, I propose that we focus on supporting each other in the North Sea. I will support your move from YOR to ENG, and you support my move from BRE to PAR. This way, we both secure our interests in the channel and put pressure on Germany.

Germany, noted on your western push. I can help keep the south contained so you don't have to look back. I am moving toward Venice this fall to pressure Italy and secure Trieste's rear. If you can spare support from Tyrolia into Venice, we can take the center together. Otherwise, I will still move in and hold the line. What do you say?
